title: SEC4: on_event error message inconsistent with code comment
description: api.rs lines 158-164: the comment says 'Rc::get_mut succeeds because this is the only strong reference to the inner client at this point (no async task is currently holding a clone of the Rc)' but the error message returned to JS says 'cannot register callback while a request is in flight'. These two descriptions model the failure differently — the comment frames it as a memory-safety invariant (ownership), while the error frames it as a concurrency state. In practice both are true (an in-flight request holds an Rc clone), but a maintainer debugging the failure will be confused by the mismatch. Fix: align the comment with the error: // Rc::get_mut fails when an async send_message/send_whisper task holds an Rc clone; // that only happens while a request is in flight.
